title: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers vote to authorize strike as contract negotiations stall
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers in Brookline voted to authorize a strike after weeks of stalled contract negotiations.
The union, recognized in June 2024, cited job protection and concerns over self-service kiosks as key issues.
More than 80% of union members supported strike authorization; negotiations with management remain ongoing.

title: Alex Cora believes this Red Sox player will slug 30 home runs this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Alex Cora predicts Red Sox outfielder Wilyer Abreu will hit 30 home runs in the 2026 MLB season.
FanGraphs projects no Red Sox player to hit 20 or more home runs in the 2026 season.
Wilyer Abreu hit 22 home runs in the 2025 season despite missing games due to injury.

title: New NFL mock draft has Patriots taking ‘fascinating’ offensive tackle with pick No. 31
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots projected to draft Arizona State tackle Max Iheanachor with the 31st pick in the 2026 NFL Draft.
Iheanachor, a late bloomer from Nigeria, became a standout starter at Arizona State, earning All-Big 12 honors.
Veteran Morgan Moses, now 35, may soon need a replacement; Iheanachor offers potential with time to develop.

title: Worcester school officials address racist comments after girls basketball game
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Worcester officials condemned racist comments targeting South High players after a game against Andover on March 6.
NAACP and YWCA urged Worcester leaders to address the racial abuse directed at South High's girls basketball team.
A viral video with offensive comments suggested violence at the game, prompting widespread backlash and support for the players.

title: Rehoboth man allegedly sexually exploited minors, had ‘hundreds’ of illegal images, videos
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Christopher Mota, 29, of Rehoboth, was arrested and charged with four counts of sexual exploitation of minors.
Authorities discovered hundreds of child sexual abuse images and videos on Mota's phone during a border search in 2025.
Mota admitted to receiving illegal material from minors and made payments via Apple Cash and Cash App.

title: Jackson Square intersection reopens after burst water main flooded streets
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jackson Square intersection in Jamaica Plain reopened after a 48-hour repair of a burst water main.
The 12-inch diameter water pipe, which caused flooding, had last been restored in the 1990s.
Several businesses, including three daycares, were evacuated and closed temporarily due to the flood.

title: Former Brigham and Women’s doctor facing new rape charges
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Derrick Todd, 52, is indicted on three rape counts and 17 indecent assault counts involving 11 patients in Framingham.
Todd worked as a rheumatologist at Charles River Medical Associates and Brigham and Women's until 2023.
Over 200 former patients have joined a class action lawsuit against Todd following the new allegations.

title: Getting a vehicle ready for Van Life
author: John Paul
bullets: John Paul of AAA Northeast advises using a second battery for powering appliances in a camper van.
A pure sine wave converter is recommended for cleaner power when converting a van for remote work and living.
Adding a rooftop solar panel can help maintain and charge the van's auxiliary battery efficiently.

title: Charges dropped against former Brockton choral director after alleged victim declines to testify
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Charges against former Brockton choral director Matthew Cunningham were dropped after the alleged victim refused to testify.
Cunningham, accused of paying an underage boy for sex, was arrested in August 2025 after a police investigation.
Prosecutors dismissed the case due to lack of prosecution; Cunningham is no longer employed at Brockton Public Schools.

title: 18-year-old and 14-year-old charged in daytime armed robbery in Charlestown
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Davonte Jordan, 18, and a 14-year-old boy were arrested for an armed robbery in Charlestown, Boston.
The suspects allegedly pointed a gun at an elderly woman and stole her purse, which contained credit cards.
Jordan pleaded not guilty and is held without bail; police found the victim's credit card on the 14-year-old.
